content.text: Answer FOUR questions in detail. State carefully any results used without proof.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: that every real sequence has a monotonic subsequence

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: that every bounded real sequence has a convergent subsequence.

problem.text: Let \(T\) be a two-point metric space. Prove that the metric space \(X\) is connected if and only if each continuous function from \(X\) to \(T\) is constant.
Let \(X \subseteq \mathbb{R}\) and consider the following statements: Does (a) imply (b)? Does (b) imply (a)? Proof or counterexample for each.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: each continuous function from \(X\) to \(\mathbb{R}\) is bounded;

problem.text: Let \(f : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}\) be uniformly continuous and let \(B \subseteq \mathbb{R}\) be bounded. Must \(f(B)\) be bounded? Proof or counterexample.

The continuous function \(f : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}\) is differentiable except perhaps at the point \(a\). Decide whether \(f\) is necessarily differentiable at \(a\) if it is further assumed that \(f'(t) \to 0\) as \(t \to a\).
